props.message:
The Old Throne.

Carved before any record. Nobody alive remembers its purpose. It is empty.

The wind around it does not touch you. The seat has dust as old as the wind.

Someone is standing here.

You can't quite see them. They turn at the sound of your steps and then they are gone.

ALTITUDE MARKER

You are at the highest point in the Wyrdmark. The wind is the same wind you breathed at sea-level, only thinner, and older for the climb.

Carved deep into the rim:

"THE CROWN IS COLD AND THE BREATH IS THIN. BREATHE WHAT YOU CAN."

Memorial slab.

For those who climbed and did not return. The list is long; the names are old. We do not read them aloud, because the wind is enough.

Posted by an old hand:

"If you see something beside the Throne, do not chase it. It is being kind by leaving."

props.open_message: A pouch of summit pebbles, smoothed by wind.
A Heart Container — the climb was long, but you arrived. The Crown has a quiet way of being generous.

root.speaker: Pilgrim Hesp
root.text: Look at you, little climber. The wind up here is thin and old. I come once a year. I have come for forty years. What would you ask of an old man with cold knees?

choices.label: What do you hope to see?

why.speaker: Pilgrim Hesp
why.text: Because the climb is the answer. Because if I stop climbing I will not start again. Because the wind here is the breath of Lirien with no leaves between us.

hope.speaker: Pilgrim Hesp
hope.text: Some climbers say they have seen her. Lirien, I mean. A figure beside the Throne, or a wind that turns. I have seen nothing in forty climbs. I keep climbing.

throne.speaker: Pilgrim Hesp
throne.text: The Throne is older than the asking. There is no record of who carved it. Nobody alive remembers what it was for. That doesn't seem to bother the Throne.

bye.speaker: Pilgrim Hesp
bye.text: Walk well, climber. Breathe what you can.

root.speaker: Reader-on-pilgrimage
root.text: Pardon — I'm taking notes. You arrived. I had written that you would. The page is glad to be right.

from.speaker: Reader-on-pilgrimage
from.text: The Library. Not Sigilkeep's — the other one. We had a spring of new arrivals; the Throne has been generous this year, before the climb begins.

writing.speaker: Reader-on-pilgrimage
writing.text: What was carved here, who carved it, who came after. I always wrote what came after first; the order is wrong but the page does not mind.

bye.speaker: Reader-on-pilgrimage
bye.text: Walk well. You did walk well.

root.speaker: Lirien
root.text: *Lirien stands beside the Old Throne. Tall, hooded, looking past you to the cold horizon.* You climbed. Few do.

choices.label: I came to ask about the Door.

door.speaker: Lirien
door.text: The Door at the end of the Forge. It is held by an older watch than any kingdom. To open it you need a chord no one alive can hum alone. *Lirien glances at you.* Bring me what you have learned, and I will teach you the rest.

weave.speaker: Lirien
weave.text: *Lirien listens. Closes her eyes.* You have Glim's Theme. You have the Sun. You have the Moon. *She nods.* Then I will give you the chord that ties them.

weave_check.speaker: Lirien
weave_check.text: *She hums it once, slowly. Up... down... up... left... right. The wind carries it.* Now hum it back. The Door will hear.

remembered.speaker: Lirien
remembered.text: *Lirien tilts her head a fraction.* The Triglyph holds. The Door is yours to wake.

bye.speaker: Lirien
bye.text: *The wind on the summit does not stop for anyone.*

props.warp_message: You stand at the height. The world is small below.

load_zones.prompt: [E] Into the Hall of Lost Things
